verified_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
note: Guzzo 1991 Mol Microbiol; AprA secretion depends on apr-adjacent AprDEF genes, complementation/heterologous-export assay. Direct AprA T1SS-secretion primary for P. aeruginosa. Replaces wrong Serratia/Erwinia 1997 ref.

verified_title: Secretion of cyclolysin, the calmodulin-sensitive adenylate cyclase-haemolysin bifunctional protein of Bordetella pertussis
note: Glaser 1988 EMBO J (correct .tb03288 DOI, fixes the wrong E-cadherin .tb03249). Shows CyaA secretion requires CyaB/CyaD/CyaE T1SS. B. pertussis not B. bronchiseptica, but same cyaABDE operon/toxin; canonical CyaA T1SS-secretion primary.

verified_title: A zinc metalloprotease inhibitor, Inh, from the insect pathogen Photorhabdus luminescens
note: Valens 2002 Microbiology; states PrtA is secreted via a classical type I pathway, characterizes the prtA-inh-prtBCD operon (PrtBCD = T1SS ABC exporter). Photorhabdus-specific; replaces wrong Serratia/Erwinia 1997 ref. No earlier dedicated PrtA-secretion paper found.

verified_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
note: No P. entomophila-specific AprA secretion assay exists in PubMed. Guzzo 1991 demonstrates the AprA/AprDEF T1SS export mechanism in P. aeruginosa (homolog, family-level), not P. entomophila L48. Fixes the wrong CooB/CS1-pilin DOI; flag as family-level.

verified_title: Molecular investigation of the role of ApxI and ApxII in the virulence of Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae serotype 5
note: Reimer 1995 Microb Pathog; apxICABD-deletion mutant cannot export ApxI, complementation restores ApxI export (apxIBD = T1SS). Direct ApxI secretion primary, stronger than the Frey 1993 nomenclature ref.

verified_title: Functional characterization of a cloned haemolysin determinant from E. coli of human origin, encoding information for the secretion of a 107K polypeptide
note: Mackman & Holland 1984 Mol Gen Genet; identifies the HlyA(107K) export functions (HlyB/HlyD) by mutagenesis, HlyA not exported without them. Cleaner HlyA secretion-assay primary than the 1985 hlyCABD sequence paper.

verified_title: Mosaic structure and molecular evolution of the leukotoxin operon (lktCABD) in Mannheimia (Pasteurella) haemolytica, Mannheimia glucosida, and Pasteurella trehalosi
note: No B. trehalosi (P. trehalosi)-specific Lkt secretion assay exists. Davies 2002 J Bacteriol sequences the full lktCABD operon (incl. lktB/lktD T1SS) in P. trehalosi: correct organism but sequence/evolution, not a secretion assay. Replaces the wrong-organism Strathdee/Lo 1989 P. haemolytica ref; LktBD T1SS export is family-level across Pasteurellaceae.

verified_title: Mosaic structure and molecular evolution of the leukotoxin operon (lktCABD) in Mannheimia (Pasteurella) haemolytica, Mannheimia glucosida, and Pasteurella trehalosi
note: No M. glucosida-specific lkt secretion assay exists. Same Davies 2002 paper sequences lktCABD (incl. lktB/lktD T1SS) in M. glucosida: correct organism, sequence/evolution not secretion. Replaces the wrong-organism Highlander 1990 P. haemolytica ref; LktBD T1SS export is family-level.

verified_title: Protease secretion by Erwinia chrysanthemi: the specific secretion functions are analogous to those of Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
note: Letoffe 1990 EMBO J (PMID 2184029; Crossref 404s for this old Wiley DOI, title confirmed via PubMed). PrtDEF T1SS primary: prtD/E/F mutations abolish protease secretion in E. chrysanthemi. Demonstrated on PrtB/PrtC; PrtA shares the same PrtDEF exporter (family-level). Cleaner machinery primary than the 1997 paper.

verified_title: A zinc metalloprotease inhibitor, Inh, from the insect pathogen Photorhabdus luminescens
note: Valens 2002 Microbiology; PrtA secreted via classical type I pathway, prtA-inh-prtBCD operon (PrtBCD = T1SS ABC exporter). P. luminescens-specific; replaces wrong Serratia/Erwinia 1997 ref. prtA1 (TT01) is the same Photorhabdus serralysin/T1SS substrate.

verified_title: General secretion pathway (eps) genes required for toxin secretion and outer membrane biogenesis in Vibrio cholerae
note: Sandkvist 1997 J Bacteriol; eps (Eps T2SS) genes required for enterotoxin secretion through the outer membrane in V. cholerae, with eps mutants. Direct Eps-dependent cholera-toxin secretion primary (vs the Sikora secretome that did not detect CtxA/B).

verified_title: General secretion pathway (eps) genes required for toxin secretion and outer membrane biogenesis in Vibrio cholerae
note: Sandkvist 1997 J Bacteriol; same Eps-T2SS-dependent enterotoxin secretion evidence applies to the B subunit (CtxB; secreted holotoxin). Replaces the Sikora secretome that did not detect CtxA/B.

verified_title: A novel type II secretion system in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
note: Ball 2002 Mol Microbiol; the Hxc (second T2SS) primary. Under phosphate limitation the hxc mutant loses the LapA alkaline phosphatase from the supernatant (Hxc-dependent, Xcp-independent). Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Identification of a chitin-binding protein secreted by Pseudomonas aeruginosa
note: Folders 2000 J Bacteriol; identifies CbpD as a major secreted/extracellular protein of P. aeruginosa. Abstract does not directly test xcp-dependence (later secretomes confirm T2SS). Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Characterization of a Pseudomonas aeruginosa transposon insertion mutant with defective release of exoenzymes
note: Lindgren & Wretlind 1987 (J Gen Microbiol/Microbiology); xcp-1 Tn5 mutant fails to release multiple exoenzymes (staphylolytic/elastolytic activities cell-bound). Foundational Xcp-dependent exoenzyme secretion; LasA not individually quantified in the abstract. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Alteration of the lipopolysaccharide structure affects the functioning of the Xcp secretory system in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
note: Michel 2000 J Bacteriol; directly studies LasB (elastase) secretion via the Xcp T2SS machinery, xcpR mutant causes partial intracellular LasB accumulation. Direct Xcp-dependent LasB secretion. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Alteration of the lipopolysaccharide structure affects the functioning of the Xcp secretory system in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
note: Michel 2000 J Bacteriol; studies LipA (lipase) secretion via the Xcp T2SS alongside LasB. Xcp-dependent LipA secretion; no cleaner LipA-only Xcp primary found. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: LipC, a second lipase of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, is LipB and Xcp dependent and is transcriptionally regulated by pilus biogenesis components
note: Martinez 1999 Mol Microbiol; title/abstract state LipC export requires a functional xcp gene cluster. Direct Xcp-dependent LipC secretion. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: A secreted aminopeptidase of Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Identification, primary structure, and relationship to other aminopeptidases
note: Cahan 2001 JBC; identifies PaAP (PA2939, M28) in P. aeruginosa culture filtrate as a secreted aminopeptidase. Abstract does not test xcp-dependence (later Xcp secretome work confirms it). Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Phosphatidylcholine affects the secretion of the alkaline phosphatase PhoA in Pseudomonas strains
note: Liu 2016 Microbiol Res; PhoA exported via the Xcp T2SS, pcs mutant accumulates PhoA in the periplasm with reduced extracellular release. Shows Xcp-dependent PhoA export (strain ATCC27853 not PAO1, same pathway). Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Involvement of the twin-arginine translocation system in protein secretion via the type II pathway
note: Voulhoux 2001 EMBO J; the two studied P. aeruginosa T2SS (Xcp/secreton) substrates are the phospholipases PlcH and PlcN (Tat then secreton export). Direct T2SS-substrate evidence for PlcH. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Involvement of the twin-arginine translocation system in protein secretion via the type II pathway
note: Voulhoux 2001 EMBO J; same paper, PlcN is the second phospholipase Xcp/secreton (T2SS) substrate. Direct T2SS-substrate evidence for PlcN. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Characterization of an endoprotease (PrpL) encoded by a PvdS-regulated gene in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
note: Wilderman 2001 Infect Immun; characterizes PrpL/protease IV as an extracellular (culture-supernatant) virulence factor of P. aeruginosa. Shows secretion but does not directly test Xcp-dependence. Standard PrpL primary; replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: Characterization of a Pseudomonas aeruginosa transposon insertion mutant with defective release of exoenzymes
note: Lindgren & Wretlind 1987 (J Gen Microbiol/Microbiology); xcp-1 Tn5 mutant retains exoenzymes/exotoxin cell-bound (MeSH includes Exotoxin A). Foundational Xcp-dependent exoprotein release; ToxA periplasmic accumulation not individually quantified in the abstract. Replaces the Filloux 2011 review.

verified_title: The type II secretion system of Legionella pneumophila elaborates two aminopeptidases, as well as a metalloprotease that contributes to differential infection among protozoan hosts
note: Rossier 2008 AEM; identifies LapA (and LapB) as Lsp/T2SS-secreted aminopeptidases from secreted-supernatant analysis of L. pneumophila. Direct Lsp-dependence. Fixes the broken soil-ecology DOI (stronger than the DebRoy hint).

verified_title: Characterization of the gene encoding the major secreted lysophospholipase A of Legionella pneumophila and its role in detoxification of lysophosphatidylcholine
note: Flieger 2002 Infect Immun; plaA mutant loses the major secreted lysophospholipase A; PlaA secreted via the type II (Lsp) system in L. pneumophila. Fixes the broken soil-ecology DOI.

verified_title: ExeA binds to peptidoglycan and forms a multimer for interaction with the outer membrane secretin complex of the type II secretion system
note: No P40600-lipase substrate-focused Exe-secretion paper in PubMed. The current Li 2011 J Bacteriol ref shows A. hydrophila lipase secretion is Exe/T2SS-machinery-dependent (lipase used as the secretion readout) and is the best available primary. Keep current; verified title via EuropePMC.

verified_title: An extracellular zinc metalloprotease gene of Burkholderia cepacia
note: No direct B. cenocepacia ZmpA T2SS-dependence (gsp-mutant secretion) paper exists. Corbett 2003 (current ref) is the original zmpA characterization; T2SS secretion is homology-inferred only, B. cepacia not J2315. Keep current; flag as homology-level.

verified_title: Characterization of the pelL gene encoding a novel pectate lyase of Erwinia chrysanthemi 3937
note: Lojkowska 1995 Mol Microbiol; the PelL-specific primary (corrects the wrong PelN ref). PelL is a secreted/extracellular pectate lyase of Out-secreting E. chrysanthemi 3937; characterization-focused, no explicit PelL out-mutant secretion assay in the abstract (Out-dependence is system-level).
